setting.note: Four college friends build their adult lives in New York across some thirty years, the story circling ever closer to Jude St Francis, a gifted litigator carrying a childhood of unspeakable harm. The map holds to Lower Manhattan: a first cramped apartment on Lispenard Street in TriBeCa, the SoHo loft on Greene Street that Jude and Willem come to share, the downtown courts and the square by NYU, with a weekend house upstate off to the north.

rights.originalWork: In copyright. Hanya Yanagihara, first published 2015. This book is mapped as a cited work: we store none of its text and reproduce no passages. The places are facts and the notes are our own.
rights.textSource: None stored. A cited book reproduces no text.
rights.territoryNotes: Locations and original commentary only. No reproduction of the copyrighted text, in any territory.
rights.attribution: A Little Life, Hanya Yanagihara (2015). Locations mapped by Read the Places; not affiliated with or endorsed by the author or publisher.

palette.note: The cool slate-blue of Lower Manhattan light off the cast-iron and the river. A cited book's colour is not a quotation, because we store no text to quote; it still has to be legible, and this clears WCAG AA on paper.
